title: VxRail vCenter Extension and Marvin Plugin Health
app: Custom (VxRail Manager REST API), vCenter logs
spl:
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:plugin" earliest=-24h
| eval hb_age_sec=now()-last_heartbeat
| where plugin_state!="healthy" OR hb_age_sec>600 OR api_error_count>0
| stats latest(plugin_state) as st, max(hb_age_sec) as max_hb_lag, sum(api_error_count) as errs by cluster_id
| sort -errs
description: VxRail management extensions integrate cluster operations with vCenter. Plugin or API failures block LCM workflows and mask host issues from operators who rely on the VxRail UI.
value: VxRail management extensions integrate cluster operations with vCenter. Plugin or API failures block LCM workflows and mask host issues from operators who rely on the VxRail UI.
implementation: (1) Collect plugin and internal API health from VxRail Manager or automation probes; (2) alert on unhealthy state, stale heartbeat, or rising API errors; (3) correlate with vCenter service restarts and SSO certificate rotations.
visualization: Table (cluster plugin status), Single value (clusters with errors), Timeline (heartbeat gaps).

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
тАв Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom (VxRail Manager REST API), vCenter logs.
тАв Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vxrail` `sourcetype="vxrail:plugin"` with fields `cluster_id`, `plugin_state`, `last_heartbeat`, `api_error_count`.
тАв For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 тАФ Configure data collection
(1) Collect plugin and internal API health from VxRail Manager or automation probes; (2) alert on unhealthy state, stale heartbeat, or rising API errors; (3) correlate with vCenter service restarts and SSO certificate rotations.

Step 2 тАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:plugin" earliest=-24h
| eval hb_age_sec=now()-last_heartbeat
| where plugin_state!="healthy" OR hb_age_sec>600 OR api_error_count>0
| stats latest(plugin_state) as st, max(hb_age_sec) as max_hb_lag, sum(api_error_count) as errs by cluster_id
| sort -errs
```

Understanding this SPL

**VxRail vCenter Extension and Marvin Plugin Health** тАФ VxRail management extensions integrate cluster operations with vCenter. Plugin or API failures block LCM workflows and mask host issues from operators who rely on the VxRail UI.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vxrail` `sourcetype="vxrail:plugin"` with fields `cluster_id`, `plugin_state`, `last_heartbeat`, `api_error_count`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom (VxRail Manager REST API), vCenter logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedтАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vxrail; **sourcetype**: vxrail:plugin. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

тАв Scopes the data: index=vxrail, sourcetype="vxrail:plugin", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
тАв `eval` defines or adjusts **hb_age_sec** тАФ often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
тАв Filters the current rows with `where plugin_state!="healthy" OR hb_age_sec>600 OR api_error_count>0` тАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
тАв `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
тАв Orders rows with `sort` тАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 тАФ Validate
Compare with VxRail Manager, VxVerify, and vCenter for the same cluster scope.

Step 4 тАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (cluster plugin status), Single value (clusters with errors), Timeline (heartbeat gaps).
grandmaExplanation: VxRail management extensions integrate cluster operations with vCenter тАФ so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
hardware: Dell VxRail with integrated vCenter plugin
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
